title: Cohasset man charged after allegedly shooting family dog while hunting coyotes from his window
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Christopher Lucas, 59, was charged after allegedly shooting his family dog while hunting coyotes from his window in Cohasset.
Lucas faces charges including animal cruelty and discharging a firearm near a dwelling; he lacked a valid hunting license.
The incident was recorded on home security, and multiple firearms were seized from Lucas's residence; his firearms license was suspended.

title: Greater Boston location of Uno Pizzeria reportedly saved by general manager
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: The Revere Uno Pizzeria reopened after five weeks, following a major revamp by general manager Amalia Korom.
Korom purchased the closed location, bringing back all former employees, citing a combined 100 years of service.
Revere's Uno is the last Greater Boston location after closures due to market forces; eight remain in New England.

title: Mass. business confidence turns positive for the first time in a year
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: The AIM Business Confidence Index in Massachusetts rose to 52 in February, its first positive reading in a year.
AI-driven productivity and higher corporate profits contributed to optimism prior to a conflict-related potential downturn.
Concerns remain over energy, tariffs, and inflation despite legislative efforts to control rising costs in Massachusetts.

title: NFL analyst is not a fan of the Patriots’ reported free agent deal with Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots reportedly sign offensive guard Alijah Vera-Tucker from the Jets to a three-year, $48 million deal.
NFL analyst criticizes Patriots' decision due to Vera-Tucker's injury history, noting multiple triceps and Achilles tears.
Vera-Tucker expected to fill guard position after Patriots traded center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears.

title: TD Garden owner sues cannabis dispensary The Boston Garden
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: TD Garden owner Delaware North sues The Boston Garden dispensary for alleged trademark infringement and brand dilution.
Delaware North, a Buffalo-based company, claims the dispensary's name deceives the public and confuses commercial affiliations.
The dispensary, co-founded by Jonathan Tucker and Ivelise Rivera, argues their brand complies with trademark procedures and is unrelated to sports.

title: Former Quincy official pleads guilty to embezzling city funds for 153 pounds of steak tips, studio recordings, framed self-portrait
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Thomas Clasby Jr. pleaded guilty to embezzling funds from Quincy's Department of Elder Services, where he worked for 25 years.
Clasby used city funds for personal purchases including $5,950 in studio time, $2,200 for steak tips, and a $4,800 Toyota Prius.
Clasby faces charges carrying up to 20 years in prison for mail and wire fraud, and up to 10 years for embezzlement.

title: ‘We do feel like we’re being invaded’: Nantucket agrees to try restricted hours at popular tourist destination
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Nantucket's Select Board voted to restrict Sconset Bluff Walk hours to 8:30 a.m. - 7:30 p.m. to address privacy concerns.
Siasconset Civic Association will hire a 'bluff docent' to educate visitors and implement measures to protect the path.
Residents say the influx of tourists, driven by social media, disrupts privacy and can lead to inappropriate behaviors.

title: Queue these up: What to watch on Netflix and more in March
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "The Lego Movie," "Misery," and "Zombieland" are now streaming on Netflix in March 2026.
"The Secret Agent," "It Was Just an Accident," and "Elio" are newly available Oscar nominees on Hulu and Disney+.
Boston.com staff recommends "A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms," "Nonnas," "Merlin," and "Wonder Man" for March streaming.

title: Why he’s running the Boston Marathon in memory of family treated at Dana-Farber
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Trevor Murray, 30, from Malden, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Murray runs in memory of his father, Jeff, and sister-in-law, Grace Orzechowski, treated at Dana-Farber.
Jeff Murray, Trevor's father, ran the 2018 Boston Marathon while undergoing cancer treatment, inspiring Trevor's participation.

title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA expects nearly 1 million people for South Boston's St. Patrick's Day parade on March 15.
Red Line will operate on a rush hour schedule and may bypass Broadway Station due to crowding.
Shuttle buses to run from South Station to South Boston; bus routes 9 and 11 temporarily suspended.
